calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Compel James River Insurance Into Binding Arbitration For An Underinsured Motorist Claim And Consolidate With The Present Case
Off Calendar Jul-06-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ANNE C. GRITZER, Esq. of Edlin Gallagher Huie + Blum (Tel: 415-397-9006) for James River Insurance Company;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF KAMRAN KHAN's Motion To Compel James River Insurance Into Binding Arbitration For An Underinsured Motorist Claim And Consolidate With The Present Case.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to compel arbitration and consolidation is denied without prejudice. First, plaintiff has not properly named or served James River Insurance Co. in this action. The court therefore lacks jurisdiction to grant the requested relief. Second, even if the court rules that James River's opposition qualifies as a general appearance, plaintiff fails to demonstrate that it currently has a right to compel arbitration. The James River insurance policy has an exhaustion requirement and plaintiff fails to show exhaustion, which would trigger the arbitration obligation. (See Gritzer Decl., Ex. 1.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/RBU)
